glossary.definition: An agent execution loop that interleaves reasoning (Thought) and actions (Action) to produce observation updates from the environment, enabling dynamic goal resolution.
A function that takes an existing state and a sequence of state updates, combining them to return the new integrated state of an execution graph.
An open standard client-server protocol enabling secure integrations of external data resources and tools into language models.
A prompting and execution strategy where an agent generates a global execution plan first and then systematically executes each step to avoid local reasoning traps.

research_papers.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
research_papers.authors: Shunyu Yao, Jeffrey Zhao, Dian Yu, Nan Du, Izhak Shafran, Karthik Narasimhan, Yuan Cao

research_papers.title: Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with Large Language Models
research_papers.authors: Shunyu Yao, Dian Yu, Jeffrey Zhao, Izhak Shafran, Thomas L. Griffiths, Yuan Cao, Karthik Narasimhan
